audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for hypocrisy warning, fear/courage sayings, Spirit speech, rich fool, ravens/lilies, treasure, watchfulness, faithful steward, fire/division, and discerning the time.

verses.restored: Meanwhile, when many thousands of the crowd gathered so that they trampled one another, he began to say first to his disciples, Watch yourselves from the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
Nothing is covered that will not be revealed, nor hidden that will not be known.
Therefore whatever you said in darkness will be heard in the light, and what you spoke into the ear in inner rooms will be proclaimed on the rooftops.
I say to you, my friends: do not fear those who kill the body and after these things have nothing more to do.
I will show you whom to fear: fear the one who, after killing, has authority to cast into Gehenna. Yes, I say to you, fear this one.
Are not five sparrows sold for two small coins? Yet not one of them is forgotten before the Divine.
Even the hairs of your head have all been numbered. Do not fear; you are worth more than many sparrows.
I say to you, everyone who confesses me before humans, the Son of Humanity will also confess before the messengers of the Divine.
But the one denying me before humans will be denied before the messengers of the Divine.
Everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Humanity will be released; but the one blaspheming against the holy Spirit will not be released.
When they bring you before synagogues, rulers, and authorities, do not worry how or what you should defend yourself with, or what you should say.
For the holy Spirit will teach you in that very hour what it is necessary to say.
Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
He said to him, Human one, who appointed me judge or divider over you?
He said to them, Watch and guard yourselves from all greed, because one's life is not in the abundance of possessions.
He spoke a parable to them, saying, The land of a certain rich person produced abundantly.
He reasoned within himself, saying, What shall I do, because I have nowhere to gather my fruits?
He said, I will do this: I will tear down my barns and build larger ones, and there I will gather all my grain and good things.
I will say to my soul/life, Soul/life, you have many good things laid up for many years; rest, eat, drink, rejoice.
But the Divine said to him, Foolish one, this night your soul/life is required from you; and what you prepared, whose will it be?
So is the one treasuring for oneself and not being rich toward the Divine.
He said to his disciples, Because of this I say to you: do not worry about the life/soul, what you should eat, nor about the body, what you should wear.
For the life/soul is more than food, and the body more than clothing.
Consider the ravens: they neither sow nor reap; they have neither storeroom nor barn, and the Divine feeds them. How much more valuable are you than the birds?
Which of you by worrying is able to add a cubit to one's span of life?
If then you are not able to do even the smallest thing, why worry about the rest?
Consider the lilies, how they grow: they neither labor nor spin. Yet I say to you, not even Solomon in all his glory was clothed like one of these.
If the Divine so clothes the grass in the field, existing today and tomorrow thrown into the oven, how much more you, ones of little trust?
Do not seek what you should eat and what you should drink, and do not be suspended in anxious unrest.
For all the nations of the world seek these things, and your Father knows that you need them.
Instead, seek his reign, and these things will be added to you.
Do not fear, little flock, because your Father delights to give you the reign.
Sell your possessions and give mercy-giving. Make for yourselves purses that do not grow old, unfailing treasure in the heavens, where thief does not come near and moth does not destroy.
For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
Let your loins be girded and your lamps burning.
Be like people waiting for their lord when he returns from the wedding feast, so that when he comes and knocks, they may open to him immediately.
Blessed/flourishing are those servants whom the lord, coming, will find watching. Amen, I say to you: he will gird himself, make them recline, and come serve them.
If he comes in the second watch or in the third and finds them so, blessed/flourishing are those ones.
Know this: if the house-master had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have allowed his house to be dug through.
You also must become ready, because the Son of Humanity comes at an hour you do not expect.
Peter said, Lord, are you saying this parable to us, or also to all?
The Lord said, Who then is the trustworthy and wise steward whom the lord will appoint over his household servants to give the food allowance at the proper time?
Blessed/flourishing is that servant whom his lord, coming, will find doing so.
Truly I say to you, he will appoint him over all his possessions.
But if that servant says in his heart, My lord delays coming, and begins to beat the male and female servants, to eat and drink and become drunk,
the lord of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not know, and will cut him apart and place his portion with the untrustworthy.
That servant who knew the will of his lord and did not prepare or act according to his will will be beaten with many blows.

But the one not knowing, yet doing things worthy of blows, will be beaten with few. From everyone to whom much was given, much will be required; and from the one to whom much was entrusted, they will ask more.
I came to cast fire upon the earth, and how I wish it were already kindled.
I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how distressed I am until it is completed.
Do you think I came to give peace on the earth? No, I say to you, but rather division.
For from now on five in one house will be divided: three against two and two against three.

They will be divided father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
He also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud rising from the west, immediately you say, A shower is coming, and so it happens.
When you see a south wind blowing, you say, There will be scorching heat, and it happens.
Hypocrites, you know how to discern the face of earth and heaven, but how do you not know how to discern this time?
Why do you not judge for yourselves what is right?

As you go with your opponent before a ruler, on the way give effort to be released from him, lest he drag you to the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
I say to you, you will certainly not come out from there until you have paid the last small coin.
